title: Hierarchy of Relational Model Constraints
description: A visual breakdown of the different types of constraints that define data integrity and business rules within a relational database schema.
relationships: Domain Constraints define valid values for Attributes
Key Constraints uniquely identify Tuples within a Table
Entity Integrity ensures Primary Keys are not null
Referential Integrity ensures Foreign Keys match existing Primary Keys
Semantic Integrity enforces custom business rules
All constraints are applied to the Relational Database Schema
